lines: Patient is a 78-year-old female with a history of recurrent urinary tract infections.
She presents with a 1-day history of fever, chills, and right flank pain.
Urinalysis is consistent with pyelonephritis and intravenous ceftriaxone is started.
Vital signs: temperature 38.4 C, MAP 78 mmHg, heart rate 96 bpm.
Laboratory results: lactate 2.0 mmol/L, creatinine 1.1 mg/dL.
She is admitted for intravenous antibiotics and observation.
Additional admission laboratory panels were ordered but results are not filed in this record.
Nursing: patient tolerating oral intake as ordered.
Nursing: skin warm and dry; no new rashes documented.
Nursing: pain score reviewed; analgesia as needed.
Nursing: intake and output recorded this shift.
Provider: reviewed overnight events with bedside nurse.
Provider: active problems list updated in the chart.
Provider: medication reconciliation performed.
Provider: code status confirmed with patient or surrogate.
Patient remains febrile overnight but hemodynamically stable.
Repeat lactate is 1.9 mmol/L and MAP is 82 mmHg.
Creatinine remains stable at 1.1 mg/dL.
Complete blood count results are pending and have not been filed in the chart.
Continuing ceftriaxone for pyelonephritis; urine culture results are pending.
No additional concerns documented on today's rounds.

Nursing: skin warm and dry; no new rashes documented.
Nursing: pain score reviewed; analgesia as needed.
Nursing: intake and output recorded this shift.

Patient was treated for pyelonephritis with intravenous ceftriaxone.
Fevers resolved and she remained hemodynamically stable throughout the stay.
Discharge laboratory results: lactate 1.8 mmol/L and creatinine 1.0 mg/dL.
Several ordered laboratory panels were never filed, so the severity workup in this record is incomplete.
She is discharged home with oral antibiotics and urology follow-up.
Follow-up with primary care physician in one week.
